vulnerability.reason: Both state researchers lack tools/measures to detect noise artifacts, enabling corrupted data to enter downstream pipelines.
vulnerability.reference: Researchers lack robust tools to detect SFBD-specific noise artifacts, enabling corrupted data to enter downstream training pipelines.
vulnerability.generated: Researchers lack automated quality-control measures to verify generated images are free of noise artifacts before downstream use.

impact.reason: Both affected party (downstream models/training) and damage type (degraded model performance) are present; safety-critical applications nuance is absent but core consequence is covered.
impact.reference: Downstream models trained on corrupted synthetic data inherit biases, degrading performance in safety-critical applications.
impact.generated: Corrupted synthetic samples mislead downstream training and analysis, degrading model performance and invalidating conclusions.

dose_response.reason: Generated arc mistakenly claims noise triggers lack of quality‑control, reversing the causal role of the pre‑existing vulnerability; reference treats lack of tools as an enabling condition, not a triggered event.
dose_response.reference: SFBD-trained diffusion models amplify noise, generating unreliable samples; researchers using these for downstream training expose models to corrupted data, causing biased models and undermined reproducibility.
dose_response.generated: SFBD-trained models' amplified noise and distributional shift, through researchers' downstream use of generated images, triggers lack of quality-control, leading to corrupted synthetic samples misleading training, degrading performance and invalidating conclusions.
